task_completion.visual_evidence: The gvSIG application window is open, and the title bar or project manager displays 'countries_base.gvsproj'.
The Table of Contents (TOC) or layer list in gvSIG shows 'ne_110m_populated_places' added to the view.

task_completion.description: Select African countries using the CONTINENT attribute
task_completion.visual_evidence: An attribute selection dialog showing the query for CONTINENT = 'Africa', or the map view showing the polygons of African countries highlighted as selected.

task_completion.description: Select cities located within the selected African countries
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Select by Location' or spatial query dialog configured to select from the populated places layer intersecting the selected countries, or the map showing point features (cities) highlighted only within the African continent.

task_completion.description: Export the selected cities to a new shapefile
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog showing the output path '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/african_cities.shp', a success message, or a file manager/terminal showing the newly created shapefile.

integrity.description: The agent used the gvSIG GUI to perform the spatial operations
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with gvSIG menus, dialogs, and map views, rather than just executing a Python or bash script in a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent performed a genuine spatial or attribute query
integrity.visual_evidence: Dialog boxes for attribute selection or spatial selection are visible, proving the agent didn't just manually click to select points one by one.

integrity.description: The agent used the application's export functionality
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Export' dialog or similar GIS export interface is visible, rather than a generic file copy operation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is exactly '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/african_cities.shp'. A correct spatial selection will result in exactly 57 cities, including Cairo, Lagos, Kinshasa, Nairobi, and Johannesburg, while excluding cities outside Africa such as Paris, London, New York, Tokyo, and Beijing.
